ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for branded miniature chocolate chip cookies. FOODON_03301710 (cookie food product) is the closest defensible parent class. Branded Famous Amos product is not individually catalogued in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKK (Biscuits and cookies) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for branded miniature butter chocolate chip cookies.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for branded packaged cookie products.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 10 miniature cookies weighing approximately 30g, providing approximately 155 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Famous Amos Chocolate Chip Cookies — Singapore Nutrition Information Panel (NIP) product label; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central: Cookies, chocolate chip, commercially prepared (SR Legacy ID 18158)
nutrient_source.organization: Famous Amos Singapore (Ferrero Group) — Singapore product NIP; USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary values sourced from Famous Amos Singapore product NIP (verified label data as provided in input record, confidence 1.0). Micronutrient values cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for commercially prepared chocolate chip cookies (FDC ID 18158) to confirm plausibility. Where label data was available, label data takes precedence. Micronutrients not on the Singapore NIP (monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, water) are set to -1 as not reported.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of butter-based chocolate chip cookies — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 57 (medium) sourced from University of Sydney GI Database for butter-based chocolate chip cookies. Famous Amos cookies are not individually listed; the closest analogous entry for commercially prepared butter-based chocolate chip cookies (GI ~57) is used. The high butter fat content (29g fat/100g, 16.3g saturated fat/100g) delays gastric emptying, moderating the glycemic response relative to lower-fat biscuits. GL per 30g serving calculated as: (57 × 18.6g available carbohydrate per 30g serving) / 100 = 10.6.

hypertension.key_factor: High saturated fat (16.3g/100g) and moderate sodium (250mg/100g; 75mg per 30g serving)
hypertension.guidance: While a single 30g serving contributes only 75mg sodium (low), the high saturated fat content (16.3g/100g) and caloric density (517 kcal/100g) are cardiovascular risk factors; patients with hypertension should limit consumption to one serving and avoid the typical 100–200g mall bag portion.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium GI (57), high sugar (38g/100g), and high caloric density (517 kcal/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The medium GI of 57 and GL of ~11 per 30g serving are manageable in strict portion control, but the high sugar content (38g/100g) and caloric density make Famous Amos cookies glycemically significant at typical mall consumption volumes (100g bag = 38g sugar, 517 kcal); individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit intake to one 30g serving and account for the carbohydrate load in their meal plan.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very high saturated fat (16.3g/100g), cholesterol (35mg/100g), and ultra-processed formulation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat content of 16.3g/100g (predominantly palmitic and stearic acid from real butter) significantly exceeds recommended daily limits in a typical serving; patients with cardiovascular disease should treat Famous Amos cookies as an occasional indulgence only, strictly limiting to one 30g serving, and should not consume the full 100–200g retail bag in a single sitting.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Famous Amos Chocolate Chip Cookies are miniature, bite-sized butter-based chocolate chip cookies sold in Singapore shopping malls and supermarkets. Each cookie weighs approximately 3g; the standard serving is 10 cookies (~30g), providing 155 kcal, 8.7g fat, 18.6g carbohydrate, and 11.4g sugar. The cookies are baked using real butter (not palm oil), which gives them a characteristically rich, crumbly texture and a warm vanilla-butter-chocolate aroma that is strongly associated with Singapore mall culture — particularly at kiosks in Orchard Road, Bugis Junction, and HarbourFront. Famous Amos was founded in 1975 by Wally Amos in the United States and is now owned by the Ferrero Group (acquired 2019). The brand has been a Singapore shopping mall institution since the 1980s. Nutritionally, the cookies are energy-dense (517 kcal/100g), high in saturated fat (16.3g/100g from butter), high in sugar (38g/100g), and moderate in sodium (250mg/100g). The glycemic index is approximately 57 (medium) because the high butter fat content delays gastric emptying and moderates the glycemic response. The glycemic load per 30g serving is approximately 10.6 (medium). The mini-cookie format creates a portion illusion: 10 cookies feels like a modest snack, but a typical 100–200g mall bag purchase delivers 517–1,034 kcal and 38–76g sugar. Clinically, Famous Amos cookies are a moderate concern for Type 2 Diabetes (medium GI but high sugar density at scale), a high concern for cardiovascular disease (very high saturated fat), and a moderate concern for hypertension (low per-serving sodium but high saturated fat and caloric density). The cookies are vegetarian and halal-certified in Singapore. Allergens include wheat (gluten), dairy (butter), eggs, and soy (chocolate chips).
common_user_queries: how many calories in Famous Amos cookies

confidence_reason: Primary nutrient values sourced directly from the Famous Amos Singapore product Nutrition Information Panel (NIP) with confidence 1.0 as provided in the verified input record. Values cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for commercially prepared chocolate chip cookies (FDC ID 18158) and found to be consistent. GI sourced from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for butter-based chocolate chip cookies (GI ~57). All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are present and plausible.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown (MUFA/PUFA split) is not reported on the Singapore Famous Amos product NIP and is not available in the USDA FoodData Central branded entry for this specific product. water_g set to -1: moisture content is not reported on the Singapore product NIP. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per Famous Amos Singapore product NIP. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero for a butter-based baked cookie product with no fruit ingredients; confirmed consistent with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for commercially prepared chocolate chip cookies. All other micronutrient values are sourced from the verified Famous Amos Singapore product NIP as provided in the input record. GI of 57 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for butter-based chocolate chip cookies; Famous Amos is not individually listed in the GI database. GL per 30g serving = (57 × 18.6) / 100 = 10.6. Serving size of 30g (10 mini-cookies) is the standard Singapore NIP serving size. Halal certification applies to Singapore retail product. Country of origin for manufacturing may vary by batch; brand origin is USA (1975).
